arts.ttl: We're all enjoying watching Middlesbrough right now but it's different for Michael Carrick
arts.sum: It's with that in mind, and the in-game element of his analysis and plotting that means, unfortunately, his enjoyment has to come in a different way to us as supporters. When it’s done, it gives you a good feeling when you’ve got the right result."

arts.ttl: Michael Carrick's insight into Middlesbrough team selection planning in a three-game week
arts.sum: Michael Carrick has offered insight into his team selection thought process for a three-game week like this one, with Boro hosting Blackburn Rovers on Wednesday evening. There’s certainly an idea of what the three games might look like - which players might be suited to certain games, who we’re playing against and how that works in a three-game week.

arts.ttl: Blackburn Rovers overcoming their own squad illness for Middlesbrough after postponed game
arts.sum: Blackburn Rovers boss John Eustace is hoping his side will be fit and firing for their trip to Middlesbrough on Wednesday evening, after a recent illness in the squad had many struggling for the game on Saturday which ended up being postponed. It means when Blackburn take to the field against Boro at the Riverside, it will be their first game for over two weeks - though given the illness in their squad ahead of the weekend, it might have been a blessing in disguise.

arts.ttl: Potential Boro team vs Blackburn as Michael Carrick faces selection quandary
arts.sum: As well as assessing respective ankle issues for Hayden Hackney and Ben Doak, the Boro boss revealed that an illness, which kept Seny Dieng out of Saturday's victory over Oxford United, is afflicting other players in the squad. DC - George Edmundson - Edmundson continued his fine Boro form and, having already had the illness ahead of the international break, the hope will be that he isn't one now suffering from it ahead of this one.
